channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: A judge says the Kennedy Center must update him on its plans — and address that tarp
item.description: A federal judge has ordered the Kennedy Center to update him on programming and operational plans. But with most of the staff gone and many artists booked elsewhere, what shows would they present?
item.content:encoded:
A federal judge has ordered the Kennedy Center to update him on programming and operational plans. But with most of the staff gone and many artists booked elsewhere, what shows would they present?

(Image credit: Alex Wroblewski)
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas

item.title: Star Fox Review: Can't quite teach an old Fox new tricks
item.description: The Switch 2 Star Fox remake comes with high-effort visuals and a fun battle mode, but its campaign feels stuck in the past.
item.content:encoded:
The Switch 2 Star Fox remake comes with high-effort visuals and a fun battle mode, but its campaign feels stuck in the past.

(Image credit: James Mastromarino/Nintendo)
item.dc:creator: James Perkins Mastromarino

item.title: 4 ways to design a dreamy summer, according to a happiness expert
item.description: Don't let the season fly by. Gretchen Rubin, host of the Happier podcast, shares exercises to help you get what you want out of summer. Fill out the printable worksheet and stick it on your fridge.
item.content:encoded: Don't let the season fly by. Gretchen Rubin, host of the Happier podcast, shares exercises to help you get what you want out of summer. Fill out the printable worksheet and stick it on your fridge.
item.dc:creator: Marielle Segarra

item.title: Meet the creator of the Story Spine, an 8-sentence tool to create and analyze stories
item.description: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks to playwright Kenn Adams about his formula for creating or analyzing stories called the Story Spine.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks to playwright Kenn Adams about his formula for creating or analyzing stories called the Story Spine.
item.dc:creator: Matt Ozug

item.title: A musical wedding tradition puts Philly grooms to the test
item.description: Philadelphia is home to a unique wedding custom you've probably never heard of.
item.content:encoded: Philadelphia is home to a unique wedding custom you've probably never heard of.
item.dc:creator: Justin Kramon

item.title: Working hard as ever, Wendell Pierce aims for an annual trifecta: TV, film and theater
item.description: Pierce says he's motivated by the "ticking clock of mortality" — and the desire to challenge himself as an actor. He's currently starring in the Shakespeare Theatre Company production of Othello.
item.content:encoded:
Pierce says he's motivated by the "ticking clock of mortality" — and the desire to challenge himself as an actor. He's currently starring in the Shakespeare Theatre Company production of Othello.

(Image credit: Teresa Castracane)
item.dc:creator: Tonya Mosley

item.title: Here are the nonfiction books NPR staffers have loved so far this year
item.description: A biography of Hannibal Lecter. A meditation on trees. A memoir by a child prodigy violinist. A treatise on the way we poop. These are just a few of the nonfiction books our NPR colleagues are enjoying.
item.content:encoded: A biography of Hannibal Lecter. A meditation on trees. A memoir by a child prodigy violinist. A treatise on the way we poop. These are just a few of the nonfiction books our NPR colleagues are enjoying.
item.dc:creator: Meghan Collins Sullivan

item.title: NPR staffers share their favorite fiction reads of 2026 so far
item.description: Facts by day, fiction by night! At the end of a long day in the newsroom, many of our journalists head home and escape into novels of all types.
item.content:encoded: Facts by day, fiction by night! At the end of a long day in the newsroom, many of our journalists head home and escape into novels of all types.
item.dc:creator: Meghan Collins Sullivan

item.title: A college student found safety in his housemate in a moment of crisis
item.description: While in his first year at college, Stephen Parker turned to his housemate while in the throes of a panic attack. The care he received stuck with him for years.
item.content:encoded: While in his first year at college, Stephen Parker turned to his housemate while in the throes of a panic attack. The care he received stuck with him for years.

item.title: Laverne Cox wrote her memoir because 'one more human story out there can help'
item.description: For more than a decade, actor Laverne Cox been one of the most visible trans women in America. But the Orange Is the New Black star says she spent most of childhood keeping herself hidden.
item.content:encoded:
For more than a decade, actor Laverne Cox been one of the most visible trans women in America. But the Orange Is the New Black star says she spent most of childhood keeping herself hidden.

(Image credit: Dimitrios Kambouris)
item.dc:creator: Tonya Mosley
